inline_comments.message:
Given you're primarily trying to increase the size of the instance to get it to build properly I would suggest doing the following:

Remove all the current node definitions and set just one at the same level as your project variable below.

Additionally, instead of jumping up to such a large instance, let's step to the next size which would be 'centos7-basebuild-4c-4g' If that fails for whatever reason, we can step up to the 8c-8g instance, but I would continue to use the centos7-basebuild flavor and not the ubuntu1604-docker flavor since that instance is targeted at running docker, not being a maven build host.

Hi Andy,

Those are good points!  I'll change the configuration and submit another version momentarily.  The reason I put the node over-ride at the job definition level was that I didn't think I could define it higher in the project template based on the openstack jjb schema that I was looking at.  

But you know this stuff better than I do, so I'll make that change and flip over to a slightly smaller centos jenkins slave and hopefully that will unblock us.

regards,

Dave Adams.
Some extra white space that should be cleaned up.

I was looking over the rendered jobs and noticed that they weren't being named aai-sparky-fe-<job> like they should. That's because you don't have your project correctly named here. It should be aai-sparky-fe if you don't do that you won't ever be able to find your jobs in Jenkins since we don't have a  tab that would find them for you.
This variable should be build-node not node
